text: I wish "sonder" had a cousin for objects: a word for the sudden awareness that a thing you own has quietly outlived its purpose and become only habit. The mug you no longer like but reach for anyway. The route you drive past the better one. We have no name for that gentle inertia, the way familiarity calcifies into choice without our noticing. Naming it might loosen its grip, or at least let us greet it. Until then it stays unspoken, doing its patient work in the corners of every ordinary, unexamined day.
